svg.title: MedLynq user journeys — TPA clerk, CFO, doctor
svg.text: User journeys — TPA Clerk · CFO · Doctor
Each journey shows persona, stages, screens, emotional arc, and the MedLynq feature each step touches. Read left to right.
Journey 1 · TPA Clerk · daily query resolution + Packet Builder

g.text: Tracks Priya's feeling at each step. Up = confident · Down = stressed.
The thing that turns 25 minutes into 45 seconds. Lynq pre-runs steps 1-3 before Priya even opens the case.
Becomes strategic when the data is visible and ready.

svg.text: Journey 3 · Doctor · OPD to claim signature

g.text: Stays focused on patient when AI handles the admin.
All three personas touch the same patient folder (PatientName_MRN). The doctor captures evidence; the TPA clerk resolves any insurer query; the CFO sees the financial outcome.
No double work — Sarah's voice note becomes the case sheet Priya cites in a packet response, which becomes a settled claim Sameer sees as ₹ unlocked.
TPA clerk journey · what it proves
• Packet Builder turns 25 min → 45 sec by pre-running steps 1-3 before the clerk even opens the case.
• Doctor signature happens on mobile in < 10 seconds, not by emailing PDFs back and forth.
• Recognition + benchmarking turn ops work into a career path — reduces churn in TPA hiring.
• Mobile push at 7am is the daily hook. The CFO sees the number before the office opens.
• Network intelligence converts a one-off underpayment into a hospital-wide dispute — that's the upsell from TPA tier to CFO add-on.
• ERP/Tally sync removes the only manual step left at month-end. Stickiness via finance team integration.
• Co-pilot prevents missing-doc queries at the bedside — the upstream wedge that cuts query rate at source.
• Voice + handwriting both feed Lynq the same Dx/ICD/Rx structure — doctor never types into the system.
• Pre-auth packet auto-drafts inside the OPD flow — patient leaves with cashless approved.
• Personal benchmark gives the doctor a reason to keep using Lynq — query rate as a quality signal.
